Just now

Text:
From July 6–10 , I took part in the Kurchatov Code 2026 program at the “Vershina” Educational Centre in Vladikavkaz.
For 5 days , we worked on the PictoMir platform [https://piktomir.ru/] – solving route optimisation tasks for a virtual robot . First with logic blocks , then with Python. The goal was simple: find the shortest and most efficient path. But the real challenge came when obstacles, time limits, and multiple robots appeared.
By the end , I earned a 2nd degree diploma, received a prize package from the Kurchatov Institute, and even appeared on the regional TV channel “Iryston”
But the most important result was understanding that logistics is not just about moving goods . It’s about strategy , sequencing, and making decisions under pressure.
My recommendation: PictoMir is perfect for anyone who wants to develop logic thinking, learn the basics of programming, and understand how algorithms work real life. I highly recommend it to schoolchildren- it’s fun, challenging and genuinely useful.
📎 Try is yourself: https://pictomir.ru/
#KurchatovCode#PictoMir#Logistics#RouteOptimisation#Python#STEM
